response.justification: The candidate does not meet the minimum qualification of 8 years C++ experience — the resume shows approximately 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments (Dec 2014–Jun 2015), and C++ is absent from the skills section entirely. This is the core language of the role and the gap is disqualifying on its own. While there is genuine breadth across distributed systems (Sociomantic's high-throughput microservices), cloud tooling (AWS CDK, Kubernetes), and community mentorship (workshops, talks), none of these compensate for the fundamental C++ deficit, and there is no evidence of sustained technical leadership of a cloud or infrastructure engineering team.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with high-throughput distributed systems at Sociomantic, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency requirements
Broad polyglot background (TypeScript, DLang, Node.js, Java) with practical cloud tooling exposure (AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes) showing adaptability across stacks
Active community contributor and educator through workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects, indicating mentorship capability
concerns: C++ experience is approximately 6 months (National Instruments, Dec 2014–Jun 2015), far below the 8-year minimum requirement, and C++ does not appear in the skills section

No evidence of sustained technical leadership or formal mentoring of cloud or infrastructure engineering teams; staff-level individual contributor roles are present but team leadership is not demonstrated
Cloud compute platform depth is shallow — AWS CDK and Kubernetes usage appears in product-adjacent contexts rather than building or owning core compute infrastructure
